asset_family: composed first screen fake shot and source families
source_family: fantasy browser rpg ui frame sheet
source_family_role: slice9-safe frame, quest rail, bottom log belt, reward slots, button states, and decor overlays

recommended_canvas.background_policy: perfectly flat chroma or true transparency only

rows.purpose: all requested reusable runtime assets for this source family
rows.item_policy: one isolated component per slot with clear gutters and no composed runtime screen

prompt: Create a production source sheet for composed first screen fake shot and source families. Source family: fantasy browser rpg ui frame sheet. Role: slice9-safe frame, quest rail, bottom log belt, reward slots, button states, and decor overlays. This is a cuttable source sheet, not a gameplay screenshot, mockup, landing page, or composed UI screen. Arrange the sheet as a row_major_grid with at least 64px outer margin and 48px gutters between all visible pixels and shadows. Row 1 isolated_assets: all requested reusable runtime assets for this source family; one isolated component per slot with clear gutters and no composed runtime screen. Leave empty chroma lanes between rows and slots. Do not compose these assets into a runtime screen. Use a perfectly flat chroma background #00ff00 or true transparency; do not use gradients, shadows, glow, or texture in the background. Keep every asset isolated with generous gutters and no overlap between shadows. Keep all labels, numbers, quest text, counters, and state values blank for runtime composition. Put unique gems, badges, medallions, locks, banners, and center ornaments in separate overlay sprites, not inside stretchable bases. Use consistent perspective, lighting, material language, and line weight across the sheet.
negative_prompt: button labels, resource counters, timer values, tutorial text, debug text, game state values, unreadable fake letters, exact reference-game UI ornaments, random letters, watermarks, labels fused into reusable backgrounds, icons fused into buttons, wrong subject, weak silhouette at gameplay size, opaque chroma-key background, baked board state, one fused screenshot used as runtime UI, shape-rectangle programmer art, text or labels baked into reusable pieces, Y-up assumptions inverted in layout notes, readable text, fake letters, watermark, fused icons inside buttons, cropped silhouettes, merged components, busy stretch centers, non-flat chroma background, purple or red-blue halo on transparent edges
constraints: no fused icons inside button backgrounds
transparent or flat chroma-key background for isolated assets
no unique decoration inside slice9 stretch zones
no copied Legend, Dragon Eternity, AQW, Shakes and Fidget, or Wartune UI ornament shapes
Y-up must be preserved in layout notes and runtime translation

relevant_asset_groups.role: single composed target for the first native screen: Old Gate scene, hero, Gate Warden quest focus, route strip, wolf encounter preview, reward preview, and persistent browser-RPG frame
relevant_asset_groups.content_policy: direction target only; do not treat as one fused runtime UI image
relevant_asset_groups.coordinate_policy: composition notes and future runtime layout must remain Y-up; any screen-space conversion is boundary-only

relevant_asset_groups.role: painted Old Gate town-hub backdrop and route strip layers
relevant_asset_groups.content_policy: no UI text, no baked labels, no copied reference landmarks
relevant_asset_groups.layer_policy: background, route strip, destination plaques, and lock/quest highlights should be separable or reproducible

relevant_asset_groups.role: isolated hero, Gate Warden, and Road Wolf silhouettes with pivots for runtime composition
relevant_asset_groups.content_policy: transparent or flat chroma-key background; no text, no reference-game character copies

relevant_asset_groups.role: quest marker, route marker, lock, wolf encounter marker, ring reward, XP, and gold icons
relevant_asset_groups.content_policy: isolated icons with gutters; no numbers or labels baked into icons

relevant_asset_groups.role: ornate but readable browser-RPG frame pieces for top hero status, right quest rail, bottom log/action belt, button bases, and reward slots
relevant_asset_groups.content_policy: runtime text/icons only inside safe areas
relevant_asset_groups.stretch_zone_policy: centers and long edges must be repeatable; unique ornaments stay in corners or separate overlays
relevant_asset_groups.decor_overlay_policy: quest gems, banners, medallions, locks, and corner ornaments must be separate overlays or fixed corner pieces, never stretched through the center

final_asset_policy.notes: Code may cut, validate, pack, and compose. Code-generated/programmer art is not acceptable as final generated UI unless an explicit exception is recorded.

acceptance_checklist: Source sheet is not a full gameplay screenshot.
Background is flat #00ff00 or true transparent alpha.
No readable text, fake glyphs, labels, numbers, or watermark.
Components have clear gutters and do not share antialias/shadow pixels.
No stretchable slice9 bases are mixed into this source family.
Ornaments that should not stretch are separate overlay sprites with visible isolation.
Icons/sprites have full silhouettes with padding for alpha trim.
Source can pass source-sheet intake before crop rectangles are trusted.
Accepted output will get a generation record with provider/model/workflow/seed or no-seed reason.
